title: Download Range Timeseries Data
content:
# Download Range Timeseries Data

Returns a **CSV file** with Time Series data within the specified time range one or more resources (Asset /Data Stream pairs). Optional to preprocess and aggregate the data on the server using `agg` and `time_bucket` before downloading.

## Endpoint

`POST /timeseries/range/download`

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key")
response = client.timeseries.downloadTimeseriesRange()
print(response)

agg.description: Performs mathematical calculations on the Time Series values and returns the calculated results only. Depending on enumerator used `time_bucket` may also be required.

end_time.description: UTC time for the latest time in the Time Series, formatted in RFC 3339.

fill.description:
How to fill the values when there is no data. Valid options are:
  - `none`: Doesn't fill empty values
  - `null`: Fills empty values with a null value
  - `linear`: Fills using [linear interpolation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linear_interpolation)
  - `previous`: Fills using the previous non-null value
  - `<value>`: Provide a value to be used to fill

group_by_selector.description: If true, results will be separated per `selector` element `resource` (Asset / Data Stream pair).

order.description: Sort order of the `timestamp` key for the returned data. This string is case sensitive.

selectors.description: An array of `resources` and corresponding data `field` element name to filter on the list and optional `agg` calculations.

fields.description: A filter on the list based on the `field` element names. Blank array will return all data field element names and associated values.

resource.description: Specifies the resource (Asset / Data Stream pair) from which field/values are returned.

start_time.description: UTC time for the earliest time in the Time Series, formatted in RFC 3339.

time_bucket.description: Defines the time range to use to aggregate the data values when using the `agg` key. Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".

time_shift.description: Shift initial starting point of time buckets from the standard epoch for `time_bucket`. Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".
